{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A global logistics platform adding real-time driver earnings transparency across three jurisdictions with materially different labor classifications, legal requirements, and regulatory gatekeepers. The feature must encode jurisdiction-specific compensation display rules while maintaining a coherent platform experience.",

    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Base Pay", "definition": "The fixed component of driver compensation for accepting and completing a delivery, before any bonuses, tips, or deductions. For employees (CA), this must meet or exceed minimum wage. For contractors (Indonesia), this is the agreed-upon rate per task." },
      { "term": "Per-Delivery Bonus", "definition": "Variable compensation tied to completing individual deliveries, potentially including surge pricing, distance premiums, or volume incentives." },
      { "term": "Tip", "definition": "Gratuity paid by the customer and passed through to the driver. Must never be commingled with Base Pay or used to offset minimum wage obligations in employee jurisdictions." },
      { "term": "Deduction", "definition": "Any amount subtracted from gross earnings: platform fees, equipment charges, insurance contributions, tax withholdings, or penalty charges. Each deduction must have a category, amount, and legal basis." },
      { "term": "Earnings Breakdown", "definition": "The itemized, real-time view of a driver's compensation showing each component (base pay, bonuses, tips, deductions) as distinct line items with a net total." },
      { "term": "Driver Classification", "definition": "The legal employment status of the driver in their operating jurisdiction: employee (CA under AB5), employee subject to works council (Germany), or independent contractor (Indonesia).       { "term": "Works Council Approval", "definition": "Formal consent from the Betriebsrat (works council) in Germany required before any changes to compensation display systems can be deployed to German drivers. This is a legal prerequisite, not a courtesy." },
      { "term": "Wage Transparency Obligation", "definition": "A jurisdiction-level legal requirement dictating what compensation information must be disclosed to workers, in what form, and with what frequency." },
      { "term": "Net Earnings", "definition": "The final amount payable to the driver after all deductions are applied to gross earnings (base pay + bonuses + tips)." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Every earnings breakdown must display base pay, per-delivery bonuses, tips, and deductions as separate, individually identifiable line items. No component may be hidden or aggregated into another.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Commingling earnings components corrupts the data model and makes it impossible to verify legal compliance in any jurisdiction. A breakdown that merges components is structurally incorrect.",
      "testable_assertion": "For any completed delivery, the earnings breakdown contains exactly four distinct component categories, and the sum of (base_pay + bonuses + tips - deductions) equals net_earnings."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Tips must never be used to satisfy minimum wage obligations. In employee jurisdictions (California, Germany), base pay alone — before tips — must meet or exceed the applicable minimum wage floor.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Using tips to offset base pay is a wage theft violation under California and German labor law. This is a legal invariant that, if broken, exposes the platform to class-action liability and regulatory penalties.",
      "testable_assertion": "For any pay period in an employee jurisdiction, (total_base_pay / total_hours_worked) >= jurisdiction_minimum_wage, calculated without including tip amounts."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Every deduction displayed must include a human-readable description, the exact amount, and the legal or contractual basis for the deduction. Unexplained deductions are prohibited.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying a deduction without justification violates wage transparency obligations in California and German labor law, and erodes driver trust universally. A deduction without a basis is not a valid deduction — it is an unexplained loss.",
      "testable_assertion": "No deduction line item exists where description is null/empty or legal_basis is null/empty."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The earnings breakdown for California drivers must comply with California wage transparency requirements: itemized wage statements showing gross wages, total hours worked, all deductions, net wages, and the inclusive dates of the pay period.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "While currently a legal requirement, the specific fields and format could change with new legislation. The obligation to comply is invariant; the exact specification is policy-driven.",

    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "The feature must not be deployed to German drivers until works council (Betriebsrat) approval has been formally obtained and recorded. Deployment without approval is a violation of the Betriebsverfassungsgesetz (Works Constitution Act).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Deploying compensation display changes without works council consent is illegal under German co-determination law.       "testable_assertion": "The feature flag for Germany earnings transparency cannot be enabled unless works_council_approval_status = 'approved' and approval_date is non-null."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Driver classification (employee vs. independent contractor) must be resolved from the driver's operating jurisdiction, not chosen by the driver or defaulted by the platform. Classification determines which earnings rules, display obligations, and deduction categories apply.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Misclassification is the central legal risk in gig economy platforms. Allowing classification to be set incorrectly — even accidentally — cascades into wrong pay calculations, wrong tax treatment, and wrong display rules. The system is in a corrupt state if classification is wrong.",
      "testable_assertion": "For every driver, classification is derived from jurisdiction lookup and cannot be manually overridden without an audited legal-review workflow."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "In California, because drivers are employees under AB5, the platform must track and display hours worked. Earnings must be reconcilable against hourly minimum wage. In Indonesia, where drivers are independent contractors, hours tracking is not required and per-task compensation is the unit of account.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a classification-dependent policy. The underlying principle (show what is legally required) is invariant, but the specific tracking requirements are jurisdiction-policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "California driver earnings views include hours_worked; Indonesia driver earnings views do not require hours_worked but must show per-delivery amounts."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Earnings data must be displayed in the local currency of the driver's operating jurisdiction and labeled with the correct currency code (USD, EUR, IDR).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying earnings in the wrong currency is not a formatting issue — it is factually incorrect compensation information. A driver seeing their earnings in the wrong currency cannot understand what they have been paid.",
      "testable_assertion": "Every monetary value in the earnings breakdown carries a currency_code matching the driver's jurisdiction, and no cross-currency amounts appear without explicit conversion labeling."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Real-time earnings display must reflect the current state of completed deliveries. Earnings for in-progress (not yet delivered) orders must be shown as 'pending' or 'estimated' and clearly distinguished from confirmed earnings.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Showing unconfirmed earnings as confirmed is misinformation. A driver making financial decisions based on earnings that could change (e.g., cancelled order) would be harmed. The distinction between confirmed and pending is a data integrity issue.",
      "testable_assertion": "No delivery in status 'in_progress' or 'pending_confirmation' has its earnings displayed without an 'estimated' or 'pending' qualifier."
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [

    { "scenario": "Works council approval in Germany is revoked or expires after feature deployment", "expected_behavior": "The feature must be immediately disabled for German drivers.
